posts.title: Real-time Updates for System Design Interviews
posts.description: Two hops for real-time: client-server (polling, long polling, SSE, WebSockets) and source-to-server (Pub/Sub). Start simple unless you need sub-second latency.

posts.title: Multi-step Processes for System Design Interviews
posts.description: Reliable multi-step workflows: event sourcing, Temporal, Step Functions. Durable execution, automatic retries, and compensation.

posts.title: Dealing with Contention for System Design Interviews
posts.description: Fix race conditions with atomicity, locks, or optimistic concurrency. Scale to distributed coordination when multiple databases are involved.

posts.title: Caching for System Design Interviews
posts.description: Cache-aside with Redis is the default. Layer CDN for global users. Master invalidation and you cover 90% of interview caching questions.

posts.title: Managing Long-Running Tasks for System Design Interviews
posts.description: Accept immediately, process asynchronously with queues and workers. Poll or webhook for status. Retries, dead letter queues, idempotency.

posts.title: Handling Large Blobs for System Design Interviews
posts.description: Files never pass through your server. Presigned URLs, multipart upload, resumable upload, CDN for downloads.

posts.title: Scaling Writes for System Design Interviews
posts.description: Vertical scaling, sharding, queues, batching. Each tier addresses a different constraint. Start simple, escalate when math proves you need it.

posts.title: Scaling Reads for System Design Interviews
posts.description: Three tiers: optimize the database, scale horizontally with replicas and sharding, then add caching. Start simple.

posts.title: I Made Claude Prove its hallucination
posts.description: I stopped using 1 clever model and built a local multi-agent debugging workflow that had to earn every answer before I trusted it.

posts.title: Indexes Don't Make Databases Fast
posts.description: What I learned the hard way is simple. Most read-scaling pain starts with the wrong lookup path, not the wrong database.

posts.title: You Think Global SQL Is Just Cross-Region Writes… It Is Not
posts.description: Global SQL is not broadcasting writes. It is about time control and coordination - and most engineers fail because they copy the...
